Major cardiovascular events (MI, stroke, CV death) in high-CV-risk primary prevention (PREDIMED hard endpoint); cross-domain dietary-pattern lever also lowering
findings.note: Core lipid-driven 10-yr ASCVD risk (USPSTF 2022 treat threshold >=0.075 selective / >=0.10 recommend). CAC is the strongest single reclassifier; ApoB > LDL-C for discordant/residual risk. Standard car

outcomes_covered: major cardiovascular events (MI, stroke, heart failure, CV death) per 5 mmHg systolic BP reduction
findings.note: Threshold = sustained SBP >=130-140 mmHg; true BP-driven ASCVD risk graded (BPLTTC). Kept distinct from cvd_risk_ascvd because the action (antihypertensives) and effect size are separate. Ambulatory B

outcomes_covered: Major adverse cardiovascular events (composite of CV death, MI, stroke, and urgent/ischemia-driven coronary revascularization) in chronic and post-MI coronary a
3-point MACE (nonfatal MI, nonfatal stroke, CV death) in post-MI patients with residual inflammation (hs-CRP >=2 mg/L)
findings.note: Residual inflammatory risk: hs-CRP >=2 mg/L on optimized lipid lowering (mostly secondary prevention); ~30-40% of statin-treated. Standard care rarely measures or acts. Threshold expressed in mg/L.

outcomes_covered: major vascular events (MI, stroke, coronary revascularization) in patients reclassified to higher ASCVD risk by elevated Lp(a), via earlier/more-intensive LDL-l
ASCVD events (expanded MACE: CV death, nonfatal MI, nonfatal stroke, urgent coronary revascularization) via direct, large-magnitude Lp(a) reduction in establish
findings.note: Threshold = Lp(a) >=125 nmol/L (~50 mg/dL); ~20% of population. Genetically determined, lifelong, independent causal risk (HR ~1.3-1.5). Once-in-a-lifetime test. Proven action is risk-reclassification

outcomes_covered: first major adverse cardiovascular event (composite of CV death, nonfatal MI, nonfatal stroke, coronary revascularization, hospitalization for unstable angina)
findings.note: Threshold = TG 150-499 mg/dL on statin with established ASCVD or diabetes+riskfactor (REDUCE-IT phenotype). Action = icosapent ethyl; atrial-fibrillation harm is a real QALY debit.

findings.note: Heterozygous FH ~1 in 250-300; monogenic autosomal dominant, 3-4x CAD risk, premature CAD ~10 yr early. lipid_panel gives partial phenotypic detection (LDL >190); genetic confirmation enables cascade

outcomes_covered: incident type-2 diabetes in adults with prediabetes (impaired glucose tolerance + elevated fasting glucose)

outcomes_covered: cervical cancer mortality (and invasive cervical cancer incidence) via HPV-based screening with detection and treatment of precursor lesions
findings.note: High-grade CIN2+ screen-detectable precursor + occult invasive (invasive now rare due to screening). Primary HPV testing preferred. Detected finding is the treatable precursor, so action is largely PR

outcomes_covered: prostate cancer mortality (PSA screening with workup and treatment of screen-detected disease)

findings.note: Biallelic MUTYH-associated polyposis ~1/10,000; autosomal recessive, CRC risk 28x / lifetime 40-90%. Monoallelic effect modest -- only biallelic modeled as actionable finding.

findings.note: Germline TP53 ~1/5,000-20,000; lifetime cancer risk 70-100%. Enables whole-body MRI + breast MRI Toronto-protocol surveillance. Surveillance evidence moderate (observational SIGNIFY/SWEP53).

outcomes_covered: recurrent serious vascular events (nonfatal MI, nonfatal stroke, vascular death) in established ASCVD

findings.note: Secondary-prevention population defined mainly by documented event history; intensification (high-intensity statin, antiplatelet, semaglutide) is the gap, not detection.

outcomes_covered: all-cause mortality + heart-failure hospitalization (composite) in atrial fibrillation with HFrEF (LVEF <=35%, NYHA II-IV)
composite stroke / systemic embolism / cardiovascular death in nonvalvular AF (LAA closure vs warfarin); marked reduction in hemorrhagic stroke and all-cause de
findings.note: Paroxysmal AF is intermittent: single ECG misses much of it; extended ambulatory/wearable monitoring is the high-yield detector enabling CHA2DS2-VASc-stratified anticoagulation.

outcomes_covered: CV death or HF hospitalization (primary composite) in chronic HFrEF (NYHA II-IV, LVEF <=40%); all-cause mortality HR 0.84 (~16% RRR)
all-cause mortality (and sudden cardiac death) in symptomatic HFrEF (LVEF <=35-40%) on background ACEi/diuretic

findings.note: Echo quantifies LVEF and gates the four GDMT pillars plus ICD/CRT at LVEF<=35%; NT-proBNP flags it but does not measure EF.

outcomes_covered: CV death + HF hospitalization in HFpEF/HFmrEF (with or without diabetes)
findings.note: HFpEF/HFmrEF is a diagnosis of exclusion combining preserved EF, diastolic dysfunction on echo, and raised peptides; frequently undiagnosed. Only SGLT2i has positive-trial benefit.

outcomes_covered: all-cause mortality in severe symptomatic aortic stenosis (aortic valve replacement vs no valve replacement)
findings.note: Murmur on auscultation triggers echo confirmation; CT sizes the annulus for TAVR. Untreated severe symptomatic AS carries ~50% 2-year mortality, so AVR is high-value.

outcomes_covered: ipsilateral ischemic stroke (incl. operative stroke/death) at 5 years in recently symptomatic severe (70-99%, NASCET) carotid stenosis
findings.note: Time-critical: endarterectomy benefit is greatest within 2 weeks of the index event. Carotid duplex is first-line; CTA confirms before surgery.

outcomes_covered: all-cause mortality in ischemic LV dysfunction / high-risk anatomy (left-main, 3-vessel) CAD: CABG + medical therapy vs medical therapy alone
findings.note: CCTA identifies high-risk anatomy non-invasively; invasive angiography is the definitive map. Gates CABG in the subset where revascularization beats medical therapy on survival.

outcomes_covered: recurrent ischemic stroke after cryptogenic stroke with high-risk PFO
findings.note: Only actionable after a cryptogenic stroke workup excludes other causes; TEE with agitated-saline bubble study confirms a high-risk shunt that PFO closure addresses.

outcomes_covered: composite of acute limb ischemia, major amputation for vascular causes, MI, ischemic stroke, or CV death (MACE + major adverse limb events) in symptomatic PAD a
findings.note: ABI is the cheap first-line confirmatory test; PAD is frequently underdiagnosed. Detection enables dual-pathway inhibition (rivaroxaban+aspirin) and intensified secondary prevention.

outcomes_covered: AAA rupture death (aneurysm-specific mortality) via elective repair of a screen-detected aneurysm that has reached the operative threshold (>=5.5 cm or rapidly

findings.note: Silent until rupture; one-time screening ultrasound has a direct screen-to-mortality RCT (MASS). USPSTF Grade B in ever-smoking men 65-74, but real-world uptake is low.

outcomes_covered: gastric cancer incidence (and gastric-cancer mortality) in H. pylori-positive individuals
findings.note: Active H. pylori is a modifiable gastric-cancer lever; eradication reduces incidence. UBT/stool antigen are the accurate noninvasive detectors; serology cheap but cannot distinguish past from active;

outcomes_covered: ischemic stroke / systemic embolism in nonvalvular atrial fibrillation

outcomes_covered: ipsilateral / any ischemic stroke in asymptomatic severe (>=70%) carotid stenosis

outcomes_covered: incident all-cause dementia (and Alzheimer dementia) in older adults with vision-impairing cataract
findings.note: Untreated correctable vision impairment, chiefly operable cataract (Lancet-2024 modifiable dementia factor); sensory analogue of hearing_loss_actionable. Gates act_cataract_surgery_dementia -- observa
